message:
feat(distributed): add gap cursor seeding, interleaved chronological replay, and sender-sequence tracking

Main binary (distributed mode):
- New `distributed_agent_seed_gap_cursors()` seeds the gap replay cursor
  from existing local-pane gaps so agents resume gap streaming from the
  last known position after restart instead of re-sending everything
- `distributed_persist_payload()` now updates `pane_seq_by_sender` on
  WirePayload::Gap so the sender-side sequence tracker stays monotonic
  across both segment and gap payloads — prevents stale-gap re-sends
- New `DistributedReplayItem` enum unifies Segment and Gap into a single
  replay stream with `sort_key()` that orders by (timestamp, pane_id,
  type_ordinal, id) — gaps sort after segments at the same millisecond
  to preserve causality
- New `distributed_agent_flush_interleaved_deltas()` merges segment and
  gap deltas into chronological order before streaming, replacing the
  previous two-pass approach that could deliver gaps out of order relative
  to the segments they bracket

E2E test:
- Update distributed streaming assertions to match new interleaved
  replay behavior and gap cursor seeding

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
